data.title: Iran Launches Missiles at Israel Amid Trump's White House Speech
data.description: On April 6, Iran's state television reported that Iran has launched missiles targeting Israel. According to BlockBeats, this announcement coincided with U.S. President Donald Trump's speech at a White House event. The development marks a significant escalation in tensions between Iran and Israel, raising concerns about regional stability.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Maintains MAGA Support Amid Iran Warning
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that he continues to have strong backing from the MAGA movement, emphasizing the community's support for his actions. According to NS3.AI, Trump also issued a warning to Iran, asserting that the country would face significant consequences if it does not comply with demands. He reiterated his stance that Iran must not be allowed to possess nuclear weapons.

data.title: White House Economic Advisor Comments on Oil Price Trends
data.description: White House economic advisor Kevin Hassett has stated that U.S. President Donald Trump views the recent increase in oil prices as a temporary situation. According to NS3.AI, Hassett also recommended that individual states consider measures to manage gasoline prices effectively.

data.title: Bitcoin Gains Over 3.5% Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: Bitcoin experienced a rise of more than 3.5% on Monday, driven by adjustments in prediction markets such as Polymarket and Kalshi, which are recalibrating conflict odds amid ongoing geopolitical tensions. According to NS3.AI, these platforms are being utilized by professional crypto desks for real-time macro risk monitoring. Fabian Dori, chief investment officer at Sygnum Bank, highlighted the strategic use of these markets in assessing macroeconomic risks.

data.title: CFTC Chairman: Today's court ruling confirms the CFTC's exclusive regulatory authority over trading in designated contract markets.
data.description: Mike Selig, Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), stated in a post on the X platform that the U.S. Third Circuit Court of Appeals today reaffirmed Congress's legislative intent, confirming the CFTC's exclusive regulatory authority over Designated Contract Markets (DCMs) trading and rejecting the New Jersey gaming regulator's attempt to challenge this authority. Selig had previously pointed out that U.S. federal law clearly stipulates that the derivatives market is regulated by the CFTC. Whether it's sports, politics, or oil prices, under the federal regulatory framework, the CFTC has clear jurisdiction to regulate the forecasting market and has an obligation to uphold the regulations.

data.title: Trump: We haven't lost MAGA support; if Iran doesn't yield, we'll have nothing.
data.description: US President Trump stated that he has not lost the support of "Make America Great Again" (MAGA), and the MAGA group supports what he is doing. If Iran does not yield, there will be nothing. If Iran does not compromise, there will be no bridges and no electricity. The American people want us to win and come home. Trump reiterated that Iran cannot possess nuclear weapons. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bloomberg: Binance's compliance team has undergone personnel changes, with the chief compliance officer possibly considering leaving.
data.description: Binance's compliance team is experiencing personnel changes, affecting key positions such as financial crime monitoring and sanctions compliance. Chief Compliance Officer Noah Perlman is discussing his future departure with management and may leave this year or next. Perlman joined Binance in 2023, responsible for rebuilding the sanctions enforcement and anti-money laundering system, a key corrective measure following the company's guilty plea for violations of US regulatory rules. Binance responded that there is "no timetable for his departure and no successor has been identified," and stated that he remains focused on his current work. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Galaxy to Utilize Broadridge's ProxyVote for Onchain Shareholder Voting
data.description: Galaxy has announced its plan to employ Broadridge's ProxyVote platform for its upcoming annual meeting and shareholder vote in May. According to NS3.AI, this move marks Galaxy as the first company to leverage the expanded platform for onchain shares. Broadridge has revealed that ProxyVote now accommodates both traditional and tokenized securities. Initially, the service will record governance votes on the Avalanche blockchain, with plans to extend support to additional chains in the future.

data.title: Federal Appeals Court Rules Kalshi's Markets Under CFTC Oversight
data.description: A federal appeals court has ruled in a 2-1 decision that Kalshi's sports-related markets are subject to oversight by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), rather than New Jersey gambling law. According to NS3.AI, this decision upholds a preliminary injunction issued after New Jersey regulators sent Kalshi a cease-and-desist order last spring. The ruling bolsters Kalshi's position in an ongoing jurisdictional dispute that may ultimately be decided by the U.S. Supreme Court.

data.title: White House Economic Advisor Hassett: Fed Should Have Ability to Cut Rates
data.description: White House Economic Advisor Kevin Hassett has expressed confidence in the Federal Reserve's ability to lower interest rates if necessary. According to Jin10, Hassett emphasized that the Fed possesses the tools required to adjust monetary policy in response to economic conditions. His remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the U.S. economy and potential shifts in monetary policy. Hassett's statement reflects the administration's view on the central bank's capacity to manage economic challenges through rate adjustments.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Demands Iran Open Strait of Hormuz Amid Diplomatic Tensions
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump recently took to social media, using strong language to urge Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, this demand has sparked a series of mocking responses from Iranian embassies and consulates worldwide. Diplomatic missions in countries such as India, Zimbabwe, the UK, and South Africa have issued statements ridiculing Trump's remarks, highlighting the ongoing tensions between the two nations.

data.title: Hassett: Trump believes the oil price increase is temporary.
data.description: White House economic advisor Hassett stated that President Trump believes the rise in oil prices is temporary and has advised states to take action to address gasoline prices. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump: Iran's proposal is an important step, but not enough.
data.description: US President Trump stated that Iran's proposal was an important step, but not enough; he added, "I've seen every proposal. Tuesday is the deadline; we'll see." (Jinshi)

data.title: Hassett: The Fed is expected to lower interest rates after Warsh takes office.
data.description: White House economic advisor Hassett stated that he expects the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates after Warsh takes office, and that the Fed should have the capacity to do so. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian media: US action faltered, Trump extends deadline, prompting Iran to refuse ceasefire.
data.description: According to Iran's Press TV, Iran's decision to reject the US ceasefire proposal comes after a series of recent events in western and central Iran, including the failure of the US helicopter airborne operation, which further consolidated Iran's advantage. US President Trump has reportedly backed down from his previous threats and extended his repeatedly issued ultimatums. (Jinshi)

data.title: Turkey's Central Bank Reports March Inflation Trends
data.description: Turkey's Central Bank has reported a slowdown in food inflation for March. According to Jin10, the month-on-month inflation in the services sector increased due to rising transportation costs. Additionally, the fuel tax mechanism has helped mitigate the impact of inflation.

data.title: J.P. Morgan Analyst Raises Amazon Price Target Amid Strong AWS and AI Demand
data.description: J.P. Morgan analyst Doug Anmuth has increased Amazon's price target from $265 to $280, maintaining a buy rating on the stock. According to NS3.AI, this adjustment is attributed to robust growth in Amazon Web Services (AWS) and rising demand for artificial intelligence. Among the 67 sell-side analysts covering Amazon (AMZN), 96% have rated the stock as Strong Buy or Buy, with an average price target of approximately $295.

data.title: US officials: Iran's response was "too extreme," and it's unclear whether it will help advance a diplomatic solution.
data.description: A U.S. official said Iran submitted a ten-point response on Monday to a proposal to end the war, calling Iran's response "too extreme." It remains unclear whether it will contribute to a diplomatic solution. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Iran's Response to War Proposal Deemed Extreme by U.S. Official
data.description: On April 6, a U.S. official reported that Iran submitted a ten-point response to a proposal aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. According to BlockBeats, the response was described as 'too extreme,' raising uncertainty about its potential to facilitate a diplomatic resolution.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Affirms Regulatory Authority Over Derivatives Market
data.description: The Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), Mike Selig, has stated on the X platform that federal law clearly mandates the CFTC's oversight of the derivatives market. According to ChainCatcher, Selig emphasized that the CFTC has explicit jurisdiction to regulate prediction markets across various sectors, including sports, politics, and oil prices, under the federal regulatory framework. He also highlighted the CFTC's duty to uphold these regulations.
